schemas.name: Virtual Auditor
schemas.description: AI-powered CA and IBBI Registered Valuer firm offering IBBI-compliant valuations (ESOP, FEMA, Income Tax, Companies Act, SEBI, IBC, 409A, complex instruments, AIF), FEMA compliance, GST and income tax appeals, forensic accounting, company registration, and secretarial services across India.

address.streetAddress: G-131, Ground Floor, Phase 3, Spencer Plaza Mall, Anna Salai
Workafella, AK Estate, SV Road, off Veer Savarkar Flyover, Goregaon West

schemas.name: IBC Valuation — Fair & Liquidation Value
schemas.description: IBC valuation by IBBI Registered Valuer. Fair value and liquidation value for CIRP under Regulation 35. Resolution plan viability. Going concern assessment for insolvency proceedings.

schemas.name: IBC / Insolvency Valuation
schemas.description: IBC valuation by IBBI Registered Valuer. Fair value and liquidation value for CIRP under Regulation 35. Resolution plan viability. Going concern assessment for insolvency proceedings.

schemas.name: IBC / Insolvency Valuation
schemas.description: IBC valuation by IBBI Registered Valuer. Fair value and liquidation value for CIRP under Regulation 35. Resolution plan viability. Going concern assessment for insolvency proceedings.

step.text: Support CoC in evaluation of resolution plans
step.name: Step 6

mainEntity.name: Who appoints the Registered Valuer in CIRP?

acceptedAnswer.text: The Resolution Professional (RP) appoints two Registered Valuers. The valuers must be registered with IBBI under the relevant asset class. The RP may replace a valuer if the Committee of Creditors directs, for recorded reasons.

Regulation 35 requires two independent valuations to ensure reliability. The average of the two fair values and the average of the two liquidation values form the final estimates presented to the Committee of Creditors.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between fair value and liquidation value in IBC?

acceptedAnswer.text: Fair value assumes an orderly sale with a willing buyer (going concern). Liquidation value assumes a forced, distressed sale with time pressure. The gap between the two quantifies the destruction of value if resolution fails and liquidation occurs.

mainEntity.name: Can a Registered Valuer be appointed for both fair value and liquidation value?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. The same Registered Valuer determines both fair value and liquidation value. However, two different Registered Valuers are appointed independently, each determining both values.

mainEntity.name: Is Virtual Auditor experienced in IBC valuations?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. CA V. Viswanathan’s IBBI registration (IBBI/RV/03/2019/12333) under Securities & Financial Assets class specifically qualifies for IBC valuations. Our engine separately models going-concern and distressed scenarios with statistical validation.

mainEntity.name: What is the timeline for IBC valuation?

acceptedAnswer.text: Under Regulation 35A, the Resolution Professional must obtain fair value and liquidation value within 75 days of the insolvency commencement date. This is a statutory deadline — it cannot be extended. We prioritise IBC engagements and deliver within the prescribed timeline, coordinating closely with the Resolution Professional on data collection, site visits (for tangible asset valuation), and interim updates to the Committee of Creditors.
